title: FDsql / MyJQL
summary: MyJQL is a Redis-like key-value database (get/set/del) backed by five hollowed-out core modules: a free-space hash map (FSM), a paged table layer, chunked string storage on top of the table, a disk-backed B-Tree index, and the top-level MyJQL key/value API. Supporting infrastructure (file_io, buffer_pool, block, driver, and server shells) is pre-existing.
instructionPreview: Implement five core modules of the MyJQL teaching database engine: an on-disk free-space hash map (`hash_map`), a paged table storage layer (`table`), a chunked string storage layer (`str`), a disk-backed B-Tree index (`b_tree`), and the top-level key/value API (`myjql`).
instruction:
Implement five core modules of the MyJQL teaching database engine: an on-disk free-space hash map (`hash_map`), a paged table storage layer (`table`), a chunked string storage layer (`str`), a disk-backed B-Tree index (`b_tree`), and the top-level key/value API (`myjql`).

The C source tree lives under `/workspace/myjql/`. The five target files (`myjql/src/hash_map.c`, `myjql/src/table.c`, `myjql/src/str.c`, `myjql/src/b_tree.c`, `myjql/src/myjql.c`) currently contain compilable frameworks or stubs that you must turn into correct implementations. Public API contracts are defined in `myjql/include/hash_map.h`, `myjql/include/table.h`, `myjql/include/str.h`, `myjql/include/b_tree.h`, and `myjql/include/myjql.h`; do not change any declared public signatures. You may add or remove internal helper functions inside the five `.c` files.

## Modules

**Free-space map (hash map)** — `myjql/src/hash_map.c`
- Implement `hash_table_init`, `hash_table_close`, `hash_table_insert`, `hash_table_pop_lower_bound`, `hash_table_pop`.
- Data layout (`HashMapControlBlock`, `HashMapDirectoryBlock`, `HashMapBlock`) is defined in `myjql/include/hash_map.h`.
- `hash_table_pop_lower_bound(pool, size)` pops and removes one `addr` from the smallest bucket whose `bucket_size >= size`; if no suitable entry exists, return `-1`.
- `hash_table_insert` must reject duplicate `addr`.
- `hash_table_pop` will only be called with an `addr` that exists.
- Note: `myjql/src/table.c` depends on FSM correctness for `table_insert` / `table_delete`.

**Table storage layer** — `myjql/src/table.c`
- Implement `table_get_total_blocks`, `table_block_get_total_items`, `table_read`, `table_insert`, `table_delete`.
- The table owns a data file and an FSM file; `table_insert` must reuse a block from the FSM when possible, otherwise append a new `Block` page.
- `table_read` copies the stored row bytes into `dest`.
- `table_delete` must remove the row and update the FSM from the block's old free-space bucket to its new one.
- `table_init`, `table_close`, and `print_rid` may be refactored but must keep their existing signatures.

**Chunked string storage** — `myjql/src/str.c`
- Implement `read_string`, `has_next_char`, `next_char`, `compare_string_record`, `write_string`, `delete_string`, `load_string`.
- Chunk layout and macros (including `STR_CHUNK_MAX_LEN`) are in `myjql/include/str.h`; chunks are linked by `RID` as a singly linked list.
- `write_string` must support `size = 0`.
- `load_string` must NOT append `\\0` automatically.
- `compare_string_record` performs a lexicographic comparison.

**B-Tree index** — `myjql/src/b_tree.c`
- Implement `b_tree_init`, `b_tree_close`, `b_tree_search`, `b_tree_insert`, `b_tree_delete`.
- Follow the leaf/non-leaf node layout, split/merge/redistribution, and root-shrinking semantics in `myjql/include/b_tree.h`.
- When writing keys into non-leaf nodes you MUST call `insert_handler` to copy keys. When deleting or replacing keys in non-leaf nodes, use `delete_handler` / `insert_handler` as described in the header comments.
- If a key is not found, return an "empty RID" such that `get_rid_block_addr(rid) == -1`.

**MyJQL API** — `myjql/src/myjql.c`
- Implement `myjql_init`, `myjql_close`, `myjql_get`, `myjql_set`, `myjql_del`.
- Keep the B-Tree index, record table, and string table in sync for inserts, overwrites, lookups, and deletes.
- `myjql_get` returns `-1` if the key does not exist.
- `myjql_set` must overwrite any existing value for the key.
- `myjql_del` must remove the key/value pair if it exists and do nothing otherwise.

## Build

The project uses CMake. The top-level `CMakeLists.txt` at `/workspace/CMakeLists.txt` adds the `myjql` subdirectory. Standard build: `cmake -S /workspace -B /workspace/build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release && cmake --build /workspace/build`.

moduleDag.description: MyJQL is a Redis-like key-value database (get/set/del) backed by five hollowed-out core modules: a free-space hash map (FSM), a paged table layer, chunked string storage on top of the table, a disk-backed B-Tree index, and the top-level MyJQL key/value API. Supporting infrastructure (file_io, buffer_pool, block, driver, and server shells) is pre-existing.

nodes.label: Free-Space Map (hash_map)
nodes.description: Bucket-based on-disk hash map that tracks which table pages have available free space, keyed by available-slot size. Provides hash_table_init/close, hash_table_insert, hash_table_pop_lower_bound (find smallest bucket >= size), and hash_table_pop. Uses a HashMapControlBlock, an array of HashMapDirectoryBlock pages, and chained HashMapBlock pages, all stored in a BufferPool.

nodes.label: B-Tree Index (b_tree)
nodes.description: Disk-backed B-Tree that stores RID keys in fixed-size BNode pages managed through a BufferPool. Implements b_tree_init/close, b_tree_search, b_tree_insert (with split, copy-up for leaves, push-up for non-leaves), and b_tree_delete (with sibling redistribution and merge). Caller-supplied insert_handler/delete_handler manage key copies in non-leaf nodes. Does not call hash_map or str directly.

nodes.label: Table Storage Layer (table)
nodes.description: Paged row-storage layer built on Block pages and BufferPools. Implements table_get_total_blocks, table_block_get_total_items, table_read, table_insert, and table_delete, while reusing the hash_map FSM to find or return blocks by available space.

nodes.label: Chunked String Storage (str)
nodes.description: Stores variable-length strings as singly-linked chains of fixed-size chunks in a Table, each chunk fitting within STR_CHUNK_MAX_LEN bytes. Provides read_string, has_next_char, next_char, compare_string_record (lexicographic), write_string (including size=0), delete_string, and load_string. Calls table_insert and table_delete, which internally use the FSM hash map for free-space tracking.

nodes.label: MyJQL Key/Value API (myjql)
nodes.description: Top-level API implementing myjql_init/close/get/set/del. Stores keys and values in string records, stores key/value pairs in the record table, and uses the B-Tree index for key lookup and deletion.

edges.label: table_insert and table_delete pop and update FSM buckets through hash_table_pop_lower_bound, hash_table_pop, and hash_table_insert.
str stores and removes StringChunk rows through table_insert and table_delete.
myjql_get/set/del rely on the B-Tree index for key lookup, insert, and delete.
myjql stores key/value records in tbl_rec and depends on table_read, table_insert, and table_delete for record persistence.
myjql stores keys and values as chunked strings in tbl_str and uses the string layer for comparisons, reads, writes, and cleanup.
